metadata.name: Map Terrain and Symbol System
metadata.description: ASCII-based mapping symbols and terrain definitions for uDOS cartography

usage.description: Guidelines for using this terrain symbol

examples.description: Example locations where this terrain appears

priority.description: Display priority when multiple terrains overlap (higher wins)

coordinate_system.description: Each tile coordinate uses 2 letters (A-Z) + 2 digits (01-60)
coordinate_system.coverage: Each base cell represents 3° latitude × 3° longitude
coordinate_system.sub_tiles: F18:zz for zoom detail, F18:zz:xy for deep detail

data.description: Ocean or sea surface, also used for Markdown layout-safe water representation
data.usage: Default background for water areas, preserves grid layout
Default land terrain when no specific biome applies
Flat, fertile agricultural areas and grasslands

data.description: Dry/arid zones including semi-desert and dry edges
data.usage: Semi-arid regions, desert edges, dry climate zones

data.description: Full desert terrain, particularly Australian desert/land
data.usage: Major desert regions, extreme arid areas
Deciduous and mixed forests in temperate climates
Dense tropical rainforests and jungle areas
Mountain ranges, hills, elevated geographic features

data.description: Islands at medium to large scale
data.usage: Significant islands visible at map resolution

data.description: Major metropolitan areas and city cores
data.usage: Cities and urban centers override terrain symbols
Significant aviation hubs and international airports
Represents Easter Island and its famous statues
Represents the Taj Mahal in India
Represents the Great Wall of China

rendering_rules.priority_system: Higher priority symbols override lower priority in same tile
rendering_rules.city_override: Cities and wonders always override terrain
rendering_rules.water_default: 🟦 used as background for layout preservation
rendering_rules.single_symbol: Each tile displays only one symbol
rendering_rules.markdown_safe: All symbols must render correctly in markdown viewers
